spotlight.title: Found a folder that might be “%@”.

locateError.message: That folder doesn’t look like an analysed project. Pick the folder where the analysis was run, or analyse a folder fresh from the Welcome screen.
locateError.title: Couldn’t open that folder

chrome.selectProject: Select a project from the sidebar.
chrome.dragInterviewsDescription: Add interview recordings or transcripts to get started.
chrome.projectUnavailableHint: Connect the drive to access this project.
chrome.projectMovedDescription: The project folder couldn’t be found. It may have been moved or deleted.
chrome.duplicateProjectMessage: A project for this folder already exists: “%@”. You can open the existing project or create a new one.
chrome.emptyStateHint: Drag a folder here or press ⌘N
chrome.unanalysedSheetFooter: These files were added after the last analysis.
chrome.multipleSelectedHint: Select a single project to view its report.
chrome.welcomeBody: Drag a folder of interview recordings into the sidebar, or click + New Project to start.
chrome.inFlightSwitchMessage: “%@” is still being analysed. Switching projects will cancel it — you can resume from where it stopped.
chrome.dropProjectOntoProjectToast: “%@” is already a Bristlenose project — drag its source files instead.
chrome.dropOntoRunningProject: Finish or stop the current run before adding more.
chrome.dropOntoAnalysedProject: Adding extra interviews to an analysed project isn't supported yet.
chrome.dropOntoFailedProject: Use Retry on the toolbar to try this run again.
chrome.dropOntoUnreachableProject: This project's folder isn't reachable right now.
chrome.dropFolderContainsProject: This folder contains the Bristlenose project “%@”. Drag the project itself, or its source files separately.
chrome.copyDiskSpaceMessage: This copy needs %@ free, but only %@ is available on the destination volume.

welcome.subtitle: Turn a folder of interviews into a report you can share — on your laptop, nothing uploaded.
welcome.newProjectHint: Pick a folder of recordings or transcripts and start fresh.
welcome.dropFolderHint: Drag a folder of recordings or transcripts here to add it as a project.

steps.exportDetail: Read findings, export HTML or CSV.

privacy.redactionIntro: PII redaction is opt-in. It’s off by default because false positives (redacting research-relevant text) damage data accuracy. The feature uses Microsoft Presidio, an open-source text scanner, with a configurable confidence threshold (default 0.7).
privacy.actionThreshold: For sensitive data, lower the confidence threshold (try 0.5) to catch more at the cost of more false positives.

contributing.beforeBody: See the contributing guide on GitHub for development setup and code quality checks.

configReference.intro: Most settings are in the Settings window (⌘,). Environment variable names are shown below for advanced configuration.

aiConsent.title: AI-Powered Analysis
aiConsent.intro: Bristlenose uses AI to analyse your interview transcripts. When you use a cloud provider, transcript data is sent to that provider's servers for processing.
aiConsent.notUsedForTraining: Your data is not used to train AI models. See each provider's terms for their data retention policies.
aiConsent.sentToCloudLabel: Sent to your chosen cloud provider
aiConsent.sentItem1: Processed interview transcripts (with PII redaction applied first, if enabled)
aiConsent.sentItem3: Analysis requests (topic segmentation, quote extraction, theme clustering)
aiConsent.sentSummary: Data sent to cloud provider: processed transcripts, speaker codes, and analysis requests
aiConsent.localItem2: Participant names, contact info, and personal details
aiConsent.localItem3: Your edits, tags, and report customisations
aiConsent.localSummary: Data that stays on your device: recordings, personal details, and your edits
aiConsent.providersIntro: Cloud providers that may process your data:
aiConsent.ollamaCallout: Ollama runs entirely on your device. No data leaves your machine.
aiConsent.researcherResponsibility: Ensure participants have been informed that their interview data may be processed by third-party AI services, as required by your research ethics approval.

llmSettings._comment_temperatureLabel: ja/ko keep English pending native-UX-friend review — see TODO.md

ollama.description: Free, runs locally. No API key needed.

transcriptionSettings.backendHint: Auto detects the best engine for your Mac.

ollamaSetup.title: Use local AI
ollamaSetup.subtitle: Run analysis on your Mac. Your transcripts stay on your Mac.
ollamaSetup.runtimeDidNotStart: Local runtime didn’t start. After installing Ollama, launch it from Applications and try again.
ollamaSetup.errorNoInternet: No internet connection. Connect and try again — Ollama needs to download the model.
ollamaSetup.errorTimedOut: The connection timed out. Ollama’s servers may be slow right now; try again in a moment.
ollamaSetup.errorCantReach: Can’t reach Ollama. After installing, launch it from Applications and try again.

toast.removedBatchAnnouncement: Removed %d projects. Press Command-Z to undo.
toast.removeBlockedByRun: Stop the run on “%@” before removing it.
